opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I missed my transfusion, and now my heart is pounding out of my chest, I can barely lift my head, and my stomach feels incredibly tight and painful.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I have a lifelong blood condition that needs regular blood top-ups. I missed my last one a few days ago because of transport, and over the last day I've gone downhill fast — exhausted, racing heart even lying still, and a horrible dragging fullness in my belly.

symptom_narrative.onset: the extreme tiredness and racing heart came on over the last 24 hours; the belly fullness and dragging ache got severe tonight
symptom_narrative.duration: about a day, getting steadily worse
symptom_narrative.character: drained and weak all over, heart thumping and racing even at rest, and a heavy dull dragging tightness high up in the left side of my belly
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst I've felt in a long time — I can't stand up without nearly blacking out and I'm short of breath just talking
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: whole body for the weakness; the tightness and ache are up under the left ribs and across the upper belly
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant now and not letting up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: lying completely flat helps a little; sitting or standing makes me feel faint
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any movement, trying to sit up or stand, and eating even a little because I fill up straight away
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: pounding/racing heart at rest, breathlessness on speaking, feeling faint when upright, filling up after only a few bites, and a slight yellow tinge to my eyes and skin that I've had for ages
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've felt run-down before near transfusion time but never this bad or this fast
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm stuck in bed and need help to even sit up; my spouse is doing everything

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a lifelong inherited blood disorder that means I need regular blood transfusions to keep my blood count up
past_medical_history.how_managed: packed red cell transfusions every three weeks and a daily tablet to get rid of the extra iron the transfusions leave behind

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing mild yellowing of my eyes and skin

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a daily tablet to remove extra iron from my body (iron-chelation medicine)

family_history_as_reported.narrative: I was told my blood condition is inherited and runs in the family

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: missed the scheduled blood transfusion four days ago because I couldn't get transport to the clinic; spouse is at home and helping

objective_data_offered.value: heart pounding and racing even while lying still
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient's own sensation of palpitations; no measured heart rate available over chat

objective_data_offered.value: cannot stand or sit up without feeling like passing out; bedbound and needs help to move
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: spouse's description of orthostatic near-fainting; not a measured blood pressure

objective_data_offered.value: looks very pale and drained, breathless when speaking, and has a yellowish tinge to the whites of the eyes and skin
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: appearance over video; not a clinical examination

objective_data_offered.value: upper belly feels very tight, full and dragging, and fills up after only a few bites of food
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient-reported fullness/early satiety; the AI cannot palpate the abdomen
